title: Wild Lily, Prairie Fire : China's Road to Democracy, Yan'an to Tian'anmen, 1942-1989
description: Wild Lily, Prairie Fire : China's Road to Democracy, Yan'an to Tian'anmen, 1942-1989 is a book by Benton, Gregor., Hunter, Alan. The source page records removal or withholding in public libraries amid the post-2020 tightening of Hong Kong's public reading infrastructure.
descriptionParagraphs: Wild Lily, Prairie Fire : China's Road to Democracy, Yan'an to Tian'anmen, 1942-1989 is a book by Benton, Gregor., Hunter, Alan. The source page records removal or withholding in public libraries amid the post-2020 tightening of Hong Kong's public reading infrastructure.

What makes it interesting is the way a book becomes legible to officials as a political instrument rather than a neutral cultural object. As a book, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in Hong Kong. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: The source page records removal or withholding in public libraries amid the post-2020 tightening of Hong Kong's public reading infrastructure. More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

The source page records removal or withholding in public libraries amid the post-2020 tightening of Hong Kong's public reading infrastructure.
banningParagraphs: Wild Lily, Prairie Fire : China's Road to Democracy, Yan'an to Tian'anmen, 1942-1989 entered censorship debates as a book associated with politics, public argument, and state power. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around national security, political control, and political dissent.

The earliest event currently captured here is 2023-05-12 in Hong Kong, where Hong Kong public libraries and other government-managed collections removed from government-managed collections. The source page records removal or withholding in public libraries amid the post-2020 tightening of Hong Kong's public reading infrastructure. counterReadings.title: The Origins of Totalitarianism
counterReadings.author: Hannah Arendt
counterReadings.note: A foundational analysis of state terror, propaganda, and ideological conformity.

counterReadings.title: On Tyranny
counterReadings.author: Timothy Snyder
counterReadings.note: A short modern guide to resisting authoritarian politics and controlled public discourse.

counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: 387 B.C. to 1978 A.D.
counterReadings.author: Anne Lyon Haight
counterReadings.note: Useful for comparing older obscenity, heresy, and political bans with modern free-speech disputes.
